verses.text: God threatens a grievous murrain on Egypt's cattle, sparing Israel's. Beasts struck. The Lord sets a time and a difference.
God threatens a grievous murrain on Egypt's cattle, sparing Israel's. Beasts struck. The Lord sets a time and a difference.
God threatens a grievous murrain on Egypt's cattle, sparing Israel's. Beasts struck. The Lord sets a time and a difference.
God threatens a grievous murrain on Egypt's cattle, sparing Israel's. Beasts struck. The Lord sets a time and a difference.
God threatens a grievous murrain on Egypt's cattle, sparing Israel's. Beasts struck. The Lord sets a time and a difference.
The cattle of Egypt die, but not one of Israel's; yet Pharaoh's heart is hardened. Spared but stubborn. Even clear distinction does not move him.
The cattle of Egypt die, but not one of Israel's; yet Pharaoh's heart is hardened. Spared but stubborn. Even clear distinction does not move him.
The sixth plague — boils from ashes, on man and magician alike; God hardens Pharaoh. Boils. The wise men cannot even stand before Moses.
The sixth plague — boils from ashes, on man and magician alike; God hardens Pharaoh. Boils. The wise men cannot even stand before Moses.

The sixth plague — boils from ashes, on man and magician alike; God hardens Pharaoh. Boils. The wise men cannot even stand before Moses.
God could have cut Pharaoh off, but raised him up 'to shew in thee my power.' Spared to display. God's patience serves His glory.
God could have cut Pharaoh off, but raised him up 'to shew in thee my power.' Spared to display. God's patience serves His glory.
God could have cut Pharaoh off, but raised him up 'to shew in thee my power.' Spared to display. God's patience serves His glory.
God could have cut Pharaoh off, but raised him up 'to shew in thee my power.' Spared to display. God's patience serves His glory.
Hail 'such as hath not been in Egypt' is foretold; shelter your beasts and men. Forewarned. Mercy gives a way of escape.
Hail 'such as hath not been in Egypt' is foretold; shelter your beasts and men. Forewarned. Mercy gives a way of escape.
Hail 'such as hath not been in Egypt' is foretold; shelter your beasts and men. Forewarned. Mercy gives a way of escape.
Those who 'feared the word of the LORD' housed their servants; others left them in the field. Two responses. Fear of God brings men to obey.
Those who 'feared the word of the LORD' housed their servants; others left them in the field. Two responses. Fear of God brings men to obey.
Thunder, hail, and fire fall, smiting all in the field; only Goshen is untouched. Hail falls. The storm spares the land of God's people.
Thunder, hail, and fire fall, smiting all in the field; only Goshen is untouched. Hail falls. The storm spares the land of God's people.

Pharaoh confesses, 'I have sinned... the LORD is righteous'; Moses knows he will not truly fear. False sorrow. Confession without change is empty.
Pharaoh confesses, 'I have sinned... the LORD is righteous'; Moses knows he will not truly fear. False sorrow. Confession without change is empty.

The flax and barley are smitten; once the storm stops, Pharaoh sins yet more and hardens. Relapse. The reprieve only deepens his guilt.
